SELECT COUNT(*) FROM table WHERE LOWER(`Title`) LIKE '%great%mayoralty%campaign%'  Explanation:  - We use the `LOWER` function to convert all characters of the '`Title`' column into lowercase, so that we can perform case-insensitive search. - The `%` wildcard is used before and after the string `'great%'`, which means any number of characters are allowed between them. This allows us to match `Title`s like `"The Great Mayoralty Campaign"`. - Similarly, another set of wildcards (`'%campaig%'`) matches for any additional characters at the end.  This query

SELECT DISTINCT     t.`Tournament location` AS Location,     w.winners_share AS Share FROM      (         SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY `Year` ORDER BY winners_share DESC) as row_num FROM table WHERE winners_share = '7000'     ) w, table_name t WHERE      w.`Year` = t.`Year` AND w.row_num = 1
